title: 'Wife moved in with another man': Tamil Nadu father kills 2 kids, self
content: A father electrocuted his two young children before taking his own life. This tragic incident occurred in Saveriyarpuram, Tuticorin district, on Friday. The man was reportedly distraught after his wife left him six months prior. Police discovered the bodies after his father alerted neighbours to a locked door. An investigation into the deaths is currently underway by local authorities.

title: Gurgaon engineer stabs girlfriend to death, kills self by jumping in front of train
content: An AI engineer allegedly stabbed his girlfriend to death in Gurgaon. He then died by suicide by jumping in front of a train. The woman's family reported her missing after they could not contact her. Police discovered the woman's body in the man's PG accommodation. Investigations are ongoing to determine the relationship and motive behind the incident.

title: 'Religion is outside our scope': Annamalai charts political future for his 'movement'
content: K Annamalai has launched his new movement, "We The Leaders," which will focus on governance and public responsibility. This organization explicitly excludes religion from its scope and aims to make Tamil Nadu a leading state. The movement is not built through membership drives but as a collective platform for people. Women and young people form a significant share of the movement's composition.

title: 'Never seen more confused management': Ex-cricketer blasts Gambhir's coaching staff
content: India suffered a significant 0-4 T20I series defeat against England.  Former player Mohammed Kaif expressed strong criticism of team management's decisions.  He questioned the handling of young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Sanju Samson.  England's dominant performance saw them replace India as the top T20I side.  The teams now prepare for an upcoming ODI series.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Yastika Bhatia scripts history, becomes first-ever woman cricketer to...
content: Yastika Bhatia achieved her first Test century at Lord's Cricket Ground. She became the first woman to score a Test hundred at this historic venue. Bhatia reached the milestone on Day 3 of the ongoing Test match. Her composed innings ensured England remained under pressure throughout the game. This landmark century secured her place in cricketing history.

title: Stock market this week: Earnings, inflation data to steer D-Street
content: The upcoming week will see investors focus on corporate earnings and key economic data. India's inflation figures and foreign exchange reserves are scheduled for release soon. Global developments, including West Asia tensions, will also influence market sentiment. Foreign institutional investors have recently turned net buyers of Indian equities. Market participants will monitor monsoon progress alongside these important economic indicators.

title: Offshore wind farms could wirelessly charge electric ships, but may drive away animals
content: Offshore wind farms are expanding, and their effects on marine ecosystems are being studied.  Early research showed harbour porpoises avoided wind farm sites, reducing their activity.  Later studies indicated a gradual increase in porpoise presence, possibly due to habituation or food availability.  New computer simulations suggest construction alone did not cause significant porpoise displacement.  Scientists continue to examine underwater noise, vessel traffic, and habitat changes for long-term impacts.

title: Baldwins pull Hamptons home after cutting price from $29mn to $18.99mn
content: Alec and Hilaria Baldwin have again removed their Hamptons home from the market. The property was recently relisted at a reduced price of $18.99 million.  This marks several attempts over the past few years to sell the Amagansett residence.  The family cited their children's attachment and plans for a new adventure.  They will now divide summers between Spain and their Hamptons property.

title: Why gold never tarnishes: Scientists find the secret on its surface
content: Recent scientific research has revealed that the surface atoms of gold can rearrange themselves to create a protective barrier against oxidation. This extraordinary natural mechanism significantly reduces tarnishing rates by up to a trillion times, ensuring gold retains its radiant luster. This discovery opens up possibilities for industrial applications, allowing gold to be utilized as an even more efficient catalyst.

title: NY Mayor Zohran Mamdani revives ‘Click-To-Cancel’ rule opposed by Comcast, Disney and others
content: New York City has enacted a new 'Click-to-Cancel' law for subscriptions.  This rule requires businesses to allow easy cancellation of services.  It takes effect on October first, making the city a pioneer.  A related proposed rule will address upfront junk fees for consumers.  These new regulations aim to protect New Yorkers from hidden charges.

title: 'Will our home still be standing tomorrow?' Northeast relives its annual flood heartbreak
content: Northeast India faces severe monsoon floods and landslides, affecting nearly 50,000 people in Assam. Rising waters have submerged villages and damaged agricultural land, impacting livelihoods. Across the region, homes and infrastructure have suffered extensive damage from heavy rainfall. Thousands have been displaced and are seeking refuge in the relief camps.

title: How a New York construction scare exposed the challenges of converting offices into homes
content: A New York City office conversion project faced a setback after steel columns buckled. This incident underscores the significant engineering challenges in repurposing older buildings for housing. Cities are increasingly pursuing these conversions to address housing shortages and underutilized office spaces. Experts suggest this event may lead to stricter reviews for similar adaptive reuse projects. However, the strategy remains vital for sustainable urban development and addressing housing needs.

title: Bazball no more: England sack McCullum as Test coach days after Stokes retirement
content: McCullum's exit brings an end to the Bazball era, despite England's review of the Ashes series earlier this year backing both him and ECB managing director Rob Key.

title: How to book an entire Indian Railways train with IRCTC's FTR service
content: Indian Railways provides a unique Full Tariff Rate scheme for group travel. Individuals and organizations can now book entire trains or coaches for exclusive use. This service is ideal for weddings, religious trips, and corporate tours. Advance booking is required, with applications submitted through the IRCTC portal. Costs vary based on route, distance, and train composition.

title: 'Maybe he doesn't know': Bellingham hits back as coach says England were 'lucky'
content: England booked their place in the last four in Miami, with Bellingham scoring both goals. While the players celebrated on the pitch, Tuchel made it clear in his post-match interview that he was not satisfied with the overall display.

title: Explained: What is UN's 'right to adequate housing' and why it matters
content: The United Nations views housing as a basic human right, not just an investment. Financialization and global pressures displace millions, making cities unaffordable for many. Marginalized groups face significant discrimination in securing adequate housing worldwide. Legal foundations for this right exist in international human rights treaties. Adequate housing requires security, affordability, habitability, and access to services.

title: 'Won't rest till they're hanged': Kejriwal on Ram Mandir donation row
content: Arvind Kejriwal escalated his political attack on the BJP, linking opposition to Sundarkand recitation with demonic tendencies. He performed a Sundarkand Path, vowing punishment for those who allegedly looted donations. The BJP dismissed the event as a political gimmick and theatrics by a "political Hindu." Delhi BJP president Harsh Malhotra stated Kejriwal invokes religion only during election periods.

title: Speeding Ferrari crashes into apartment gate in Hyderabad’s Jubilee Hills
content: A speeding Ferrari Roma worth over Rs 3.5 crore crashed into an apartment gate. The luxury car sustained extensive damage after ramming the property's entrance. No injuries were reported from the accident which occurred on Sunday. The driver tested negative for alcohol and possessed a valid license. The building owner declined to file a complaint, so no FIR was registered.

title: Stakeholders: PM, CMs removal clause 'constitutionally problematic'
content: A parliamentary panel noted concerns about the anti-corruption bill's arrest clause. Stakeholders found removing officials after thirty days constitutionally problematic. The draft report suggests a sunset clause for automatic reversal of barring. It also proposes using "suspension" instead of "removal" to avoid stigma. The committee's recommendations will be tabled in Lok Sabha soon.

title: Middle East ceasefire dead as US bombs Iran, Hormuz shuts, missiles fly over Gulf
content: The United States carried out a third round of strikes on Iran after accusing Tehran of attacking a commercial container ship in the Strait of Hormuz. In response, Iran announced that it had once again closed the strategic waterway, stepped up attacks on US military facilities across the Gulf and warned that any retaliation would invite a "severe response."

title: When myths become diagnoses: Why mental illnesses remain so misunderstood
content: Despite increasing awareness of mental health, public comprehension still lags behind scientific advancements. Misunderstandings surrounding schizophrenia, dissociative identity disorder, and bipolar disorder lead to widespread stigma. The media often dramatizes these issues, portraying affected individuals as violent or erratic. To combat this, we need to prioritize education and foster empathy, while robust support systems must aid patients and caregivers in their journey toward recovery and respect.

title: When S. Janaki rejected the Padma Bhushan in 2013, sought Bharat Ratna
content: S. Janaki, the iconic Nightingale of South India, has sadly passed away at the age of eighty-eight in Mysuru. Known affectionately as Janaki Amma, she famously rejected the Padma Bhushan in 2013, arguing that recognition for artists should come while they are still alive. Janaki passionately believed her legacy warranted the Bharat Ratna, highlighting the importance of timely recognition in the arts.

title: 'Rs 1 lakh a month is dangerous': Creator says it could become a growth trap in 2026
content: A content creator argues a ₹1 lakh monthly salary is dangerous. This income offers enough comfort to reduce ambition and personal growth.  Such financial stability can lead to complacency and a career plateau.  The discussion highlights whether comfort discourages seeking new challenges.  Mindset, not just earnings, influences continued personal development.

title: Gold, silver rate outlook: Middle East conflict, inflation data to drive prices this week
content: Precious metals are expected to remain under pressure in the coming week. Geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East weighs against upcoming inflation data. Investors are awaiting economic indicators that could shape global interest rate expectations. Bullion prices ended the week sharply lower, mirroring global trends. Analysts believe bullion will remain range-bound until stronger triggers emerge.

title: Congress out of Punjab poll race, says BJP leader Fatehjung Singh Bajwa
content: A Punjab BJP leader stated the Congress party is not in the race for 2027. He believes the party will struggle to retain its existing eighteen seats. Congress leaders themselves allege compromises with the current state government. Party officials deny any internal factionalism and express unity for upcoming polls. Unity is crucial for the party, but compromised leaders should be avoided.

title: How a doctor continues to rock India without missing a beat
content: Palash Sen, frontman of Delhi-based band, Euphoria, recounts his capital life and explains why the music changed

title: BCCI discussing Doeschate & Morne Morkel's future as India coaches; T Dilip under fire
content: Indian cricket board may need new support staff after England tour concludes. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate and fast bowling coach Morne Morkel are considering leaving. VVS Laxman will lead a separate coaching team for the Zimbabwe series. Fielding coach T Dilip is also under scrutiny due to team performance. The board is discussing options to resolve these potential staff departures.

title: How an Indian man turned 'missed calls' into million dollar business
content: Apurva Shrivastava, an engineer from MIT, has successfully created a company valued at an astonishing 8,300 crore rupees. His innovative startup, Avoca, harnesses the power of AI voice agents to streamline communication for home service businesses, enhancing appointment scheduling and quote follow-ups. With over $125 million raised, Avoca's valuation has reached $1 billion. Shrivastava forecasts a transformative impact of AI on the home services sector in the near future.

title: US housing bill becomes law without Trump's signature: What's in it & what's missing
content: In a significant move to tackle the housing affordability crisis in America, a new bipartisan law known as the '21st Century Road to Housing Act' was enacted without the signature of President Donald Trump. This all-encompassing legislation focuses on increasing housing supply and lowering costs for the average citizen, while also limiting the ability of large investors to acquire multiple single-family homes.

title: 3 feared drowned as truck plunges into Ganga in UP's Kannauj
content: A truck broke through bridge railings and plunged into the Ganga river. Three people are feared drowned and one person sustained injuries in the incident. Police and disaster response teams immediately launched a search and rescue operation. Efforts are continuing to locate the missing occupants of the vehicle. The exact cause of the accident is still under investigation.

title: 'I'm devastated': Indian record holder Tejas Nandakumar barred from CWG 2026
content: India's national record holder Tejas Nandakumar has been ruled out of the 2026 Commonwealth Games despite qualifying, after his para sport classification failed to meet eligibility requirements. Speaking exclusively to TimesofIndia.com, the 20-year-old reveals how administrative delays, despite months of international travel and personal expense, shattered his Glasgow dream, while urging systemic reforms to prevent similar heartbreak for future para athletes.

title: Badrinath temple donation theft: U'khand govt orders action against officials
content: Uttarakhand government ordered action against officials for financial irregularities. An inquiry found advance funds released without proper approval from the temple corpus. Separately, a Special Investigation Team is probing alleged donation theft at Badrinath temple. CCTV footage shows a suspended employee suspiciously collecting cash from the donation room. The investigation continues with witness statements and evidence analysis.

title: Search for food takes 4 kids to begging on streets after family dispute
content: A family dispute left four young cousins without parental care and they left home. Two sisters and their cousin were found begging near Safdarjung Hospital in Delhi. The other two sisters were located begging in Maharashtra after boarding a train. Police registered a kidnapping FIR and circulated their details nationwide. The children were safely reunited with their families after the extensive search.

title: Did you know July is named after Julius Caesar?
content: Julius Caesar's legacy extends beyond his famous "Veni, Vidi, Vici" military triumphs. His Gallic Wars conquests significantly expanded Roman territories and wealth for the Republic. Caesar's reforms modernized the Roman state and introduced the Julian Calendar. His assassination on the Ides of March led to further civil war and the Roman Empire. His influence continues to be studied and inspires titles and artistic works.

title: Abhishek Sharma enters record books with bizarre milestone in India's England defeat
content: Abhishek Sharma achieved a unique T20I record during India's fifth match. He played his fifty-third T20I without appearing in Tests or ODIs. England secured a dominant series victory by winning the final match. Jos Buttler's exceptional century powered England's high score. India's bowlers and fielders struggled throughout the disappointing encounter.

title: Tripura CM opens new public hospital in Agartala
content: Tripura presently has one government-run medical college and one dental college; there are also a society-run medical college and a private medical college.

title: Real culprits being protected in Ram Temple donation embezzlement case: Congress MP
content: Senior Congress leader Pramod Tiwari reiterates demand for a Supreme Court-monitored probe into the matter

title: ESIC to add seven more healthcare facilities
content: “These medical facilities, worth approximately ₹668 crore, will cater to the healthcare needs of around 53 lakh beneficiaries across the country,” says Union Labour Ministry

title: Telangana BJP chief accuses Congress govt. of delaying Kaleshwaram rehabilitation
content: Claims delayed restoration of the Kaleshwaram barrages has hurt irrigation and drinking water supply across Telangana

title: Nehru Trophy Boat Race’s annual grant, prize money increased
content: Vishnunadh said the annual grant was being enhanced following a request by Congress MP K.C. Venugopal

title: SSF literary fest concludes
content: The four-day festival hosted literary discussions, public debates, and cultural programmes centred on media, literature and contemporary social issues

title: Rayanapadu Railway Station set for inauguration after ₹35 crore upgrade, says Vijayawada MP
content: Kesineni Shivnath (Chinni) says the project reflects Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s vision of providing world-class facilities at railway stations across the country

title: Teacher suspended after student’s hand fractured in alleged caning
content: The school management suspended the teacher after he was arrested following a complaint lodged by the child’s parents

title: Tamil Nadu drew lower quantity of rice than its share under Antyodaya Anna Yojana in five of last seven years
content: From 2019-20 to 2025-26, it was during the COVID-19 pandemic year (2020-21) that the drawal was almost close to the allocated figure of around 6.99 lakh tonnes, with the off-take being about 98%. In the remaining four years, it varied from around 73% during 2022-23 and 2023-24 to 79% during 2024-25

title: Delivery executive arrested for forcing entry into woman’s home, flashing at her
content: The accused has been identified as Vijay Mallikarjun Kamath (22); he has been arrested in connection with the incident that came to light after the survivor shared her ordeal and video footage of the confrontation on social media

title: Naidu calls on Pawan at Mumbai hospital, wishes him speedy recovery
content: The Deputy Chief Minister is recovering from his operation for rotator cuff injury on his right shoulder

title: Singer Janaki cremated with state honours in Mysuru
content: Janaki’s granddaughter Apsara Vydyula performed the last rites as per Andhra Brahmin traditions in the presence of her close relatives, people’s representatives, friends, and a large number of admirers and the general public

title: INL asks Congress to clarify stand on Kerala CM’s ‘special consideration’ for Sangh leadership
content: Kasim Irikkur alleges that Chief Minister is undermining the secular values long upheld by Kerala society

title: Standard KAVACH commissioned on Solapur–Wadi section of Central Railway
content: 149.476-km Solapur–Wadi route becomes first in the Solapur Division of Central Railway to get indigenous train protection system

title: Hyderabad SI booked after letting six-year-old granddaughter drive, claims he was preparing her for Guinness World Record
content: Video shows the child behind the wheel on the busy Gandhamguda–Bairagiguda road as traffic piles up behind the car; vehicle already had six pending traffic challans worth ₹3,800

title: Karnataka requests Kerala to donate captive elephant to Mookambika temple
content: Karnataka Chief Minister writes to his Kerala counterpart to facilitate “the donation of a female elephant from any willing temple in Kerala.” Kerala had earlier turned down a request by the Telangana CM for camp elephants. Rules are in place for transfer of elephants within or outside a State.

title: PM Modi’s Statehood promise should carry weight: Omar Abdullah
content: National Conference has announced a sit-in at New Delhi’s Jantar Mantar on July 20, the first day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, to demand restoration of Statehood and constitutional safeguards

title: Thrissur archdiocese warns against ‘attempts to malign Church’ amid nurses’ agitation
content: Catholic leaders allege vested interests are using the strike to target Church-run hospitals and the archdiocese

title: KSRTC Budget Tourism Cell to launch Karkidakam pilgrimage packages
content: Curated itineraries include Nalambala Yatras, Pancha Pandava Temple darshan featuring traditional Aranmula Vallasadhya, and trips to the shrines of Guruvayur and Mookambika

title: New medicine OP opened at Pulayanarkotta
content: This is one of the steps being taken by the government to reduce overcrowding at the MCH and to ensure that patients do not end up lying on the floor

title: KSRTC to cap passengers on Gavi bus service amid safety concerns
content: Move comes amid mounting safety concerns over passengers being stranded deep inside the forest following repeated bus breakdowns

title: India, Canada conclude third round of talks on proposed trade pact
content: The talks include trade in goods, services, intellectual property, rules of origin, sanitary and phytosanitary measures, and technical barriers to trade; the two sides seek to increase bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030

title: Kavitha’s Telangana Rakshana Sena defends party name, seeks personal hearing before ECI
content: Cites multiple ‘Aam Aadmi’, ‘Praja Sena/Praja Seva’, Republican Party variants to argue that similarity in names alone cannot justify rejection

title: Teachers’ union questions reconstitution of Mahatma Gandhi University Boards of Studies
content: Office-bearers of Congress-backed Kerala Private College Teachers’ Association (KPCTA) say Boards of Studies were hastily reconstituted just before LDF government demitted office by favouring Left sympathisers over more qualified and experienced academics

title: Panel formed to seek Kalabhavan quick redevelopment, reopening
content: Writers, artists and intellectuals take exception to the inordinate delay in redeveloping the Mallikarjun Mansur Kalabhavan in Dharwad

title: Ground set to start construction of Palakkad-Kozhikode greenfield highway
content: Over 95% of the land acquisition has been completed for the ₹8,000-crore Bharatmala project, which passes mostly through the rural areas of Palakkad, Malappuram and Kozhikode districts

title: Man shot dead in Manipur’s Kangpokpi
content: Haolal Singsit, a resident of Govajang village in the Kuki-majority district, was working on a paddy field when the incident occurred

title: Kerala Devaswom Minister criticises “excessive intervention” by Kerala High Court in temples
content: He says there is no freedom for the Devaswom Board or the government to take independent decisions in Sabarimala as the High Court is directly giving instructions even on ritual matters

title: Hassan hotel owner honours S. Janaki with a book written in 2013
content: H.S. Ravi, a small hotel owner in Hassan, gathered information about S. Janaki and wrote a book titled ‘Kannada Kampinali Gaana Kogile Dr. S. Janaki’, highlighting her contributions to film music

title: Iltija says Mehbooba Mufti and her put under ‘house arrest’,  on the eve of Martyrs’ Day
content: July 13 is commemorated as Martyrs' Day in Jammu and Kashmir as a tribute to the 22 persons who were killed by the Dogra army outside Srinagar's central jail in 1931. The L-G administration had dropped the day from the list of gazetted holidays in 2020.

title: Self-Immolation, Protests: Youth-Backed Nepal Government Faces Gen-Z Fire
content: This is the first major challenge against the government led by Prime Minister Balen Shah, who had supported the fierce Gen-Z protest last year that ended the reign of KP Sharma Oli and his team

title: Man Killed After Speeding SUV Hits Him In Delhi, Driver Arrested: Cops
content: A speeding SUV fatally struck a 45-year-old man as he walked home with a friend on the roadside after finishing work in Rajouri Garden, and police arrested the driver and seized the vehicle, officials said on Sunday.

title: Delhi's AQI Touches 261, Highest In Over 85 Days: Weather Office
content: Delhi's air quality deteriorated to 'poor' on Sunday, with the Air Quality Index (AQI) touching 261, the highest in over 85 days.

title: How Russia Built A Spy Network In Heart Of Japan
content: Ukrainian government estimates cited by the NYT suggest that around 90 per cent of Russian missiles and drones contain parts made in Japan.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US Military Says Traffic "Flowing" In Strait Of Hormuz
content: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: Ladakh Creates 17 New Tehsils To Cut 300-Km Trips For Government Services
content: Ladakh has created 17 new tehsils, taking the total from 15 to 32, to make five newly created districts fully functional and bring government services closer to people living in remote and border areas.

title: "Irresponsible Acts": Oman Summons Iran Ambassador After Latest Attack
content: Oman has also issued a formal protest to the Iranian ambassador following an attack on the country, state media reported.

title: 3 Astronauts, 1 Kerala District: Incredible Space Story From God's Own Country
content: Kerala has ample reason to celebrate. Palakkad district can now look with pride at a remarkable trio.

title: Ukraine's Zelensky Proposes Replacing Prime Minister In Government Reshuffle
content: Zelensky said the government changes were needed to "ensure the implementation of an updated political strategy", but gave no further details.

title: Mystery Of Masked Man At Ali Khamenei's Funeral Finally Solved
content: A man wearing a black face mask and a black baseball cap was seen in the front row during the funeral prayers.

title: 5 Minors Stab Man To Death In Delhi Over Accidental 'Mud Splash', Detained
content: Victim Bansi Lal was walking through a muddy stretch when he accidentally splashed mud on a group of boys passing by, who then stabbed him multiple times.

title: Delivery Agent Who Barged Into Bengaluru Woman's Home, Flashed Her, Arrested
content: The woman alleged that the delivery agent had visited her house to deliver a package and then asked her if he could use the toilet.

title: Vietnam Police Arrest Captain After Speedboat Overturns, Killing 15 Indians
content: The incident occurred on Saturday as the boat carrying 32 Indian tourists, three Vietnamese crew members and a tour guide overturned in rough seas off May Rut Ngoai islet.

title: He Fought 3 Wars For India. His "4th Battle" Is To Save His Own Land
content: The former soldier said that after serving in three wars, he never imagined he would have to make rounds of government offices to save his land.

title: Pak Woman, Son Arrested In Karnataka For Hiding Nationality In Documents
content: A criminal case has been filed against the two under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), the Foreigners Act, and the Representation of the People Act

title: 'If Not Delhi, Should We Go To White House For Statehood?': Omar Abdullah
content: Omar Abdullah further accused the BJP of systematically weakening opposition parties across the country.

title: Bangladesh Terror Probe Exposes Martial Arts Organisation, Student Leader
content: Students and instructors of Fatah Combat System (FCS), a martial arts training institution, have been arrested in the anti-terror probe in Bangladesh

title: E20 controversy explained: Why an old roadmap is back in spotlight
content: Aastha Jha is a writer at The Times of India, covering politics. She also writes on business, global affairs, social issues, with a focus on policy, governance and their impact on everyday lives. Her work brings together reportage and analysis, tracking developments in India and abroad while examining the broader social and economic currents shaping public discourse. title: Speedy completion of UAPA trials a challenge, says CJI Suryakant
content: chief justice Surya Kant says expeditious completion of trial in these cases is desirable . he says final adjudication of evidence would address grievances of non-grant of bail despite long period of incarceration .

title: Sachin at Sharjah, Kohli at MCG: Why we keep returning to cricket
content: Abhishek Asthana is a tech & media entrepreneur. He runs a creative agency called GingerMonkey & is also the Co-founder of Knot Dating, a VC funded matrimony startup. Rest of the time he tweets from the handle @Gabbbarsingh & pretends to take interest in everything under Read More

title: 'So sweet I'll read it twice': Iran celebrates Graham's death as Trump, Netanyahu mourn
